'''Level 88''' was a worker's district and housing community located in the [[Corellian Sector (Nar Shaddaa)/Legends|Corellian Sector]] of the Smugglers' Moon, [[Nar Shaddaa/Legends|Nar Shaddaa]]. Many of the denizens of Level 88 found legitimate work as freight haulers and mechanics, but there were just as many who earned their fortune through the level's various casinos.

Several [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] refugees, including [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]] and his family, lived on Level 88 from [[4 BBY/Legends|4]]-[[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]].

==Siege on Nar Shaddaa==
Shortly before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] used sections of Level 88's warehouse district for storage of contraband weaponry. They would conceal the weapons in crates of spice and secretly ship them off-world in [[blockade runner]]s in the dead of night. A young [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] named [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]] learned of this and sold the information to a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] named [[Spurch Goa]]. Goa, in turn sold the information directly to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. The [[Battle of Nar Shaddaa (Galactic Civil War)|ensuing battle]] was brief, but fierce.

[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] sent two camouflaged [[Gamma-class assault shuttle/Legends|assault shuttles]] to Level 88 and laid waste to the warehouse district. [[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] disembarked from the shuttles and engaged twenty Rebel commandos in front of the ruins of the warehouse. The commandos turned the tide of battle in their favor by firing a [[C4-CZN ion field gun]] at the shuttles destroying them. Through the course of the battle, several buildings in the warehouse district were set on fire. A second wave of Imperials, possibly led by Vader himself, arrived soon afterwards. Their "[[Death Engine]]" did even more damage, causing explosions which could be felt as high as [[Level 92]].
